title: Michigan Data Wire — July 8, 2026
teaser: Senate Democrats file hyperscale data-center bills; Oracle exemption hearing today.

lines.text: Senate Democrats today introduced a package of bills aimed at regulating hyperscale data centers across Michigan.
The measures include water-use caps, renewable-energy requirements, labor standards, and new transparency rules for large facilities.
Bridge Michigan reports the package is the first coordinated legislative response to the rapid growth of these projects.
Lawmakers will decide next steps in committee before any floor votes.
A new statewide report also recommends fresh state policies to address public concerns over energy, water, and local impacts.
Crain's Detroit Business notes the recommendations are intended to ease worries as multiple projects advance simultaneously.
In Oakland County, data-center developer Verrus has offered Lyon Township a ten-million-dollar community-benefits package tied to a proposed 1.8-million-square-foot facility.
Crain's Detroit Business reports the offer comes while township officials continue their review of the project.
In Washtenaw County, Oracle is seeking a property-tax exemption for its expanding data-center project in Saline Township.
The company applied for an Industrial Facilities Tax Exemption Certificate, and the township board will hold a public hearing today.
MLive reports the hearing is scheduled for the July 8 board meeting, giving residents a direct opportunity to comment.
Also in Washtenaw County, Lima Township residents and officials have halted a proposed 1.4-gigawatt natural-gas plant.
Michigan Advance reports the project was eyed near a large data-center site; Consumers Energy stated there is no direct link after public pushback.
Listeners can attend the Saline Township Board of Trustees meeting tonight for the public hearing on Oracle's tax-exemption application.
Full sources and updates are available at the Michigan Data Center Tracker.
